rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component type annexes for assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for bindings.
Implementation-level signal routing should be expressed via assignments in the implementation scope after subcomponents and connections are defined.
generic_rules: Use only identifiers that are explicitly declared or visible in the current AADL scope.
Avoid encoding AADL properties like timing or resource values as AGREE variables unless they are explicitly typed ports or constants.
Do not assume event ports are numeric or Boolean values; only use data values explicitly provided in the model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference component ports only when those ports are valid in the scope of the component type.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level assignments in component type annexes.
Do not encode Boolean or event port values as numeric literals unless the model explicitly defines such mappings.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations as AGREE variables
Event port numeric encoding without explicit data values
Boolean data range comparisons over opaque types
